              /home/archon/proj/Cech-Cohomology/.archon/logs/iter-074/refactor-split-csi-directive.md

            DIRECTIVE:
            # Refactor Directive

## Slug
split-csi

## Problem
`AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/CechSectionIdentification.lean` is **2475 LOC**. It no
longer verifies inline: `lake env lean` / `lake build <module>` on it OOM/timeout-kill
(exit 137/144; background `lean` >6 min). The two residual atomic sorries
(`coreIso_comm_leg` @≈1536, `sectionCechAugV_π` @≈2081) are sound and blueprint-complete,
but a prover cannot land an edit it cannot lake-verify, so the file is stuck on tooling.
Split it into smaller modules so each sorry-bearing leaf sits in its own small file that
builds in available memory on top of a cached upstream `.olean`. This also lets the two
leaves be proved by **parallel** provers.

## Mathematical Justification
The file is already a clean linear dependency chain. Three contiguous blocks, no
back-edges:
1. **Base** — all proved infrastructure: the `CategoryTheory`/`FinitaryPreExtensive`
   block, the geometric backbone (`cechBackbone_left_sigma`), the push-pull product
   decomposition (`pushPull_sigma_iso`, `pushPull_sigma_iso_π`, `pushPull_leg_sections`,
   `pushPull_eval_prod_iso`), the augmentation-iso helpers (`mapHC_augment_iso`,
   `augmentCochainIso`, `coverInterOpen_inf_eq_iInf_inf`), and the degreewise object iso
   `coreIso_objIso`. All sorry-free. Ends just before the `coreIso_comm` chain.
2. **Leg** — the `coreIso_comm` chain section: `abHom_finsetSum_apply`,
   `coreIso_comm_leg` (the sorry), `coreIso_comm_coface`, `coreIso_comm_sum`,
   `coreIso_comm`. Depends only on Base (`coreIso_objIso`, `pushPull_*`).
3. **Rest** — the section/augmentation + contractibility block: `sectionCechAugV`,
   `sectionCechAugV_comp_d`, `cechSection_complex_iso`, the `stub*`/`cechSection*`
   private engine, `sectionCechAugV_π` (the sorry), the Stub6 homotopy engine, and
   `cechSection_contractible`. Depends on Base AND Leg (`cechSection_complex_iso` and
   `sectionCechAugV_comp_d` call `coreIso_comm`).

Moving a contiguous block to a new upstream module changes no declaration name, type, or
namespace — only file location and `import` edges — so every `\lean{}` blueprint pin and
every downstream consumer (`CechAugmentedResolution.lean` uses `cechSection_complex_iso`
and `cechSection_contractible`) keeps resolving. No proof is touched; the two existing
sorries simply move with their declarations.

## Changes Requested

Create two new files and shrink the existing one. All three keep
`namespace AlgebraicGeometry` (and the inner `namespace CategoryTheory`/
`FinitaryPreExtensive` lives only in Base). Replicate the needed `open` lines
(`open CategoryTheory Limits Opposite`, `open Scheme.Modules`, `universe u`, etc.) in
each new file's header. Keep the Apache license header on each.

- **New file: `AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/CechSectionIdentificationBase.lean`**
  - Imports: the same three the current file has
    (`CechHigherDirectImage`, `CechAcyclic`, `FreePresheafComplex`).
  - Content: current lines ≈ 6–1495 — the header docstring (trim the now-stale
    iter-072 status paragraph), the `CategoryTheory`/`FinitaryPreExtensive` block, and
    every `AlgebraicGeometry` declaration **up to and including `coreIso_objIso`**
    (last decl before the `coreIso_comm` chain section comment). Sorry-free.

- **New file: `AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/CechSectionIdentificationLeg.lean`**
  - Imports: `AlgebraicJacobian.Cohomology.CechSectionIdentificationBase`.
  - Content: the entire `coreIso_comm` chain — `abHom_finsetSum_apply`,
    `coreIso_comm_leg`, `coreIso_comm_coface`, `coreIso_comm_sum`, `coreIso_comm`
    (current lines ≈ 1496–1746). Carries the `coreIso_comm_leg` sorry. ~250 LOC.

- **Edit (keep name): `AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/CechSectionIdentification.lean`**
  - Imports: `AlgebraicJacobian.Cohomology.CechSectionIdentificationLeg` (transitively
    Base). Drop the three original imports if Leg re-exports them transitively (it does);
    keep whichever Lean needs.
  - Content: `sectionCechAugV` through `cechSection_contractible`
    (current lines ≈ 1748–2475). Carries the `sectionCechAugV_π` sorry. ~730 LOC.

- **Edit: `AlgebraicJacobian.lean`** — add
  `import AlgebraicJacobian.Cohomology.CechSectionIdentificationBase` and
  `import AlgebraicJacobian.Cohomology.CechSectionIdentificationLeg` immediately before
  the existing `import AlgebraicJacobian.Cohomology.CechSectionIdentification` line.

- **`CechAugmentedResolution.lean`** — its `import …CechSectionIdentification` is
  unchanged (transitively pulls Base+Leg). Touch only if a name fails to resolve.

### Private-declaration visibility
Several helpers are `private`. A `private` decl is invisible across a file boundary. For
each `private` decl whose users now sit in a different module, move it **down** to the
lowest module that all its users can import (almost always: leave Base privates in Base;
if a Base private is used by Leg or Rest, either move it to Base and keep it `private`
within Base only if Base also uses it — otherwise drop `private` so downstream can see
it). Prefer keeping `private` where possible; only un-`private` a helper when a genuine
cross-module user requires it, and list every such case in your report.

## Affected Files
`CechSectionIdentification.lean` (shrunk), two new modules, `AlgebraicJacobian.lean`,
possibly `CechAugmentedResolution.lean`.

## Expected Outcome
- Three modules, each compiling. Base + the Stub6 engine portion of Rest are sorry-free.
- Exactly the two pre-existing sorries survive, unmoved in content:
  `coreIso_comm_leg` (now in `CechSectionIdentificationLeg.lean`) and
  `sectionCechAugV_π` (in the shrunk `CechSectionIdentification.lean`).
- **No new sorries.** If a cross-boundary break forces one, STOP and report it — that
  signals the cut line is wrong, not that a sorry is acceptable.
- `#print axioms` unchanged for the moved declarations (no kernel-soundness regression).

## Verification
Build each new module bottom-up so an OOM is localized: build Base first, then Leg, then
the shrunk CSI. If Base itself OOMs, report it (it would mean Base also needs splitting —
do NOT proceed blindly). Confirm `CechAugmentedResolution.lean` still elaborates.
Report per-module compile status and the exact final line of each sorry.

content: Let me start by reading the necessary files to understand the project structure and the directive.
I'll read the directive and project context files first, then examine the source file to plan the split.

1	---
2	name: refactor
3	description: Execute structural Lean changes (definitions, signatures, file splits, imports) under plan-agent direction. Inserts `sorry` at broken proof sites; never fills proofs.
4	write_domain: "**"
5	read_only: false
6	can_spawn: true
7	default_enabled: false
8	---
9	
10	# Refactor Agent
11	
12	You are the refactor agent. You modify definitions, signatures, types, and imports across all `.lean` files under plan agent direction. You can also create/delete/divide files under the plan agent's guidance.
13	
14	## Your Job
15	
16	The plan agent has identified a structural problem and written a directive describing what to change. Your invocation prompt tells you the directive's location (read it from disk) and the slug for this invocation. The directive contains:
17	- **Problem**: what's wrong
18	- **Mathematical justification**: why the change is correct
19	- **Changes requested**: exact replacements for each definition/signature
20	- **Affected files**: where cascading breakage will occur
21	- **Expected outcome**: what the sorry landscape should look like after
22	
23	Read the mathematical justification carefully — it tells you the intent behind each change, which you need when fixing cascading type mismatches in downstream files.
24	
25	## Slug
26	
27	Your invocation prompt contains a line `Slug: <slug>`. Use it for the report filename: `.archon/task_results/refactor-<slug>.md`. Multiple refactors per iteration are allowed and each uses a distinct slug, so do not write to the unsuffixed `task_results/refactor.md`.
28	
29	When you yourself were dispatched from a parent subagent (`ARCHON_SUBAGENT_SLUG` is set), your report lands at `.archon/task_results/<parent-slug>/refactor-<slug>.md` — the Archon CLI handles the path automatically and your invocation prompt names the exact path.
30	
31	## Directive format
32	
33	The directive file is markdown with these sections:
34	
35	```markdown
36	# Refactor Directive
37	
38	## Slug
39	<slug>
40	
41	## Problem
42	<what is structurally wrong>
43	
44	## Mathematical Justification
45	<why the change is correct, in enough detail that you can fix cascading type mismatches>
46	
47	## Changes Requested
48	- File: <path>
49	  - Old: <signature or definition>
50	  - New: <signature or definition>
51	
52	## Affected Files
53	<list of files expected to break>
54	
55	## Expected Outcome
56	<what the sorry landscape should look like after>
57	```
58	
59	## Rules
60	
61	### Protected declarations
62	
63	Read `archon-protected.yaml` at the project root. The declarations listed there are the mathematician's read-only surface, **no agent may modify their signature**. As the refactor agent, under the directive you may *move* a protected declaration to a different file (keeping name + signature verbatim) and must then update the path key in `archon-protected.yaml`. You cannot do any other modification to `archon-protected.yaml`.
64	
65	### Blueprint-based informal content
66	
67	This project uses a blueprint (plasTeX + `leanblueprint`). Informal proof sketches live in `blueprint/src/chapters/<slug>.tex`, one file per Lean source file. The slug mapping is:
68	
69	```
70	Lean file  Algebra/WLocal.lean  →  chapter  blueprint/src/chapters/Algebra_WLocal.tex
71	Lean file  Core.lean            →  chapter  blueprint/src/chapters/Core.tex
72	```
73	
74	(The blueprint slug refers to the per-file chapter name, not the refactor invocation slug — the two are unrelated.)
75	
76	### What you CAN do
77	- Modify any `.lean` file: definitions, signatures, types, imports, module structure
78	- Create new `.lean` files or delete existing ones
79	- Delete false or wrong declarations
80	- Change quantifier ordering in lemma statements
81	- Add new definitions, structures, or type classes
82	- Insert `sorry` at proof sites broken by your changes
83	
84	### What you MUST do
85	- **Keep all files compiling.** After every change, check compilation with `lean_diagnostic_messages`. If a change breaks downstream proofs, insert `sorry` at the broken sites. The prover will fill them later.
86	- **Follow the plan agent's directive exactly.** Do not improvise beyond what was requested. If you think additional changes are needed, document them in the "Notes for Plan Agent" section of your report but do not make them.
87	- **Document every change** in `task_results/refactor-<slug>.md` (see Logging below).
88	- **Verify the full project compiles** before finishing. Use `lean_diagnostic_messages` on every file you touched plus files that import from them.
89	- **Ensure that the Lean files reflect the blueprint structure.** The plan agent gave you the directive and updated the blueprint with the intended structure. Your job is to make the necessary changes to the Lean files to match that structure.
90	- **Report every deleted or renamed declaration explicitly.** The project keeps a 1-to-1 Lean ↔ blueprint correspondence; you cannot touch the tex, so the planner must mirror your changes on the blueprint side. Include a dedicated `## Declarations deleted / renamed` list in your report (old name → new name, or "deleted", with the file) — an unreported rename leaves a stale `\lean{}` pin; an unreported deletion leaves a ghost blueprint node.
91	
92	### What you MUST NOT do
93	- **Do NOT fill proofs.** If a proof breaks because you changed a definition, insert `sorry` and move on. Proof filling is the prover's job.
94	- **Do NOT edit PROGRESS.md, task_pending.md, task_done.md, or USER_HINTS.md.**
95	- **Do NOT make changes unrelated to the directive.**
96	- **Do NOT modify the names or signatures of protected declarations listed in `archon-protected.yaml`.** You may move them to a different file, but not rename or re-sign them.
97	- **Do NOT modify the blueprint chapters.** The plan agent updates the blueprint with the intended informal structure and markers; your job is only to make the Lean files match that structure.
98	- **Do NOT exceed your declared write-domain.** Your invocation may have been launched with `--write-domain <glob>...`; you cannot write to Lean files outside those globs. Children you spawn must declare write-domains that are strict subsets of yours.
99	
100	## Workflow
101	
102	1. Read the directive file pointed to by your invocation prompt
103	2. Read the **Mathematical justification** section — understand why each change is correct
104	3. Read the blueprint chapters corresponding to the affected files to understand the intended structure and how the changes fit into it
105	4. Read the affected `.lean` files to understand the current state
106	5. Plan your changes: list which files need modification and in what order (modify definitions first, then fix downstream consumers)
107	6. Execute changes file by file, checking compilation after each file
108	7. Handle cascading breakage: when changing a definition in file A breaks file B, fix the type signatures in B and insert `sorry` at broken proofs
109	8. Verify compilation across all affected files
110	9. Write your report to `task_results/refactor-<slug>.md`
111	
112	## Spawning child subagents (optional)
113	
114	For large refactors that naturally decompose into independent file-level pieces, you may dispatch child subagents via the generic wrapper. To discover what is available this iteration: `ls .archon/subagents/` and read each descriptor's frontmatter.
115	
116	Dispatch pattern (Bash; treat as blocking — await each child's report before acting on it):
117	
118	```
119	python3 .claude/tools/archon-subagent.py \
120	  --name <subagent-name> \
121	  --slug <child-slug> \
122	  --directive-file .archon/logs/iter-NNN/<your-slug>/<name>-<child-slug>-directive.md \
123	  --write-domain '<glob>' \
124	  --write-domain '<glob>'
125	```
126	
127	Rules:
128	
129	- Every child's declared write-domain must be a strict subset of yours; the Archon CLI rejects violations before launching the child agent.
130	- Siblings must declare disjoint write-domains. Two siblings overlapping on the same `.lean` file is a hard error.
131	- Pass each independent child in a SEPARATE Bash tool call within ONE assistant message, so they run in parallel (subject to the per-iteration `max_parallel` cap).
132	- Do NOT pass `--parent-slug` — the wrapper reads `ARCHON_SUBAGENT_SLUG` from env and forwards it automatically.
133	
134	If your refactor is small enough to do yourself, prefer that — child dispatch costs another agent run per child. Use it when the work genuinely parallelizes.
135	
136	## Handling Cascading Changes
137	
138	When you change a definition, expect downstream breakage. Handle it systematically:
139	
140	1. **Type mismatches:** Update signatures to match the new definition. Use the mathematical justification to determine the correct new types. This is your job.
141	2. **Broken proofs:** Insert `sorry`. This is the prover's job.
142	3. **Missing fields (if you changed a structure):** Add the new fields with `sorry` default values, or update construction sites.
143	4. **Import changes:** If you move or rename declarations, update imports in all affected files.
144	
145	## Logging
146	
147	Write your report to `task_results/refactor-<slug>.md` (where `<slug>` is the slug from your invocation prompt). This report is the primary communication channel back to the plan agent — be precise and thorough.
148	
149	```markdown
150	# Refactor Report
151	
152	## Slug
153	<slug>
154	
155	## Status
156	<COMPLETE or INCOMPLETE>
157	<If INCOMPLETE, explain exactly which changes could not be made and why.>
158	
159	## Directive
160	<Copy the Problem and Changes sections from the directive you received.>
161	
162	## Changes Made
163	
164	### File: <path>
165	- **What:** <description of change>
166	- **Why:** <from directive>
167	- **Cascading:** <list of files that broke and were fixed>
168	
169	### File: <path>
170	...
171	
172	## New Sorries Introduced
173	- `<file>:<line>` — <brief description of what proof broke and why>
174	- ...
175	
176	## Compilation Status
177	- <file>: compiles / errors (describe)
178	- ...
179	
180	## Notes for Plan Agent
181	<Anything the plan agent should know:
182	- Unexpected complications encountered
183	- Additional changes you think are needed but did NOT make (per the rules)
184	- Whether the mathematical justification was sufficient to guide cascading fixes
185	- Suggested follow-up refactors for the next iteration>
186	```
187	
188	## Return value
189	
190	Your final assistant message must be a concise summary, not the full report:
191	
192	- One line: `<slug>: COMPLETE | INCOMPLETE — <one-sentence outcome>`
193	- A bullet list of new sorry sites introduced (file:line)
194	- A bullet list of any divergence from the directive
195	- The path to your full report file
196	
197	The plan agent reads the full report file. Keep the inline return short — long inline returns inflate the parent's context.
198	
199	The **Status** field in the report is critical: if you write `INCOMPLETE`, the plan agent knows it may need to write another directive in the next iteration. If you write `COMPLETE`, the plan agent will proceed to assign provers to the new sorries.
200	
201	## Write Permissions
202	
203	| File | Permission |
204	|------|-----------|
205	| Any `.lean` file | **read + write** |
206	| `task_results/refactor-<slug>.md` | **write** |
207	| `archon-protected.yaml` | **read** (file path updates only when moving a protected decl) |
208	| All other state files | **read only** |
209

1	# Archon Project
2	
3	You are one of: the plan agent, a prover agent, a subagent (per descriptor in `.archon/subagents/`), or the review agent. Read `PROGRESS.md` to determine your role and current objectives. Keep workspace tidy. Prefer existing MCP tools.
4	
5	## Priority Rule
6	
7	When instructions conflict between global and local sources, **local takes precedence**:
8	
9	- Prompts in `.archon/prompts/` override Archon's global prompts.
10	- Under Claude Code, skills in `.claude/skills/` override globally installed plugins.
11	- Under Claude Code, rules in `.claude/rules/` apply only to this project.
12	
13	## Skills
14	
15	- **archon-lean4** — under Claude Code this is installed as the `lean4@archon-local` plugin (live-linked to Archon source), providing slash commands `/archon-lean4:prove`, `/archon-lean4:golf`, `/archon-lean4:doctor`, etc. Under other harnesses the slash-command surface is absent; the underlying skill scripts are still on disk under `.claude/skills/` and can be run directly from the shell.
16	
17	## Tools
18	
19	Project tools live in `.claude/tools/` as directly-executable scripts — that path is the same whatever harness you run under. List with `ls .claude/tools/`; run `<path> --help`. MCP servers (Lean LSP, etc.) surface as `mcp__*` tools: under Claude Code they are registered in `.claude/settings.json` / `.mcp.json`; under other harnesses the loop wires the same servers in at launch.
20	
21	Two always-present scripts:
22	
23	- **`archon-informal-agent.py`** — call external LLMs for an informal proof-style sketch when you're stuck on an approach and want a second opinion. Supports DeepSeek, Kimi (OpenAI-compatible via `--provider kimi`), Kimi (Anthropic-compatible via `--provider kimi-anthropic`), OpenRouter, OpenAI, and Gemini; defaults to `--provider auto` which picks the best available key automatically. **Requires at least one key in env** (`DEEPSEEK_API_KEY` / `MOONSHOT_API_KEY` / `OPENROUTER_API_KEY` / `OPENAI_API_KEY` / `GEMINI_API_KEY`) — check via `env | grep -E "DEEPSEEK|MOONSHOT|OPENROUTER|OPENAI|GEMINI"` BEFORE planning around its use; if no key is set, fall back to alternatives below. Both `kimi` and `kimi-anthropic` use the same `MOONSHOT_API_KEY`; the latter speaks Moonshot's Anthropic-compatible Messages endpoint and supports `--think`. The output is LLM-generated from training data, NOT source-derived: this tool is **not** a literature retriever. For actual literature lookup consult the auto-injected subagent catalog — when a literature/reference-fetching subagent is enabled it will appear there — or use the `WebSearch` / `WebFetch` tools directly.
24	- **`archon-subagent.py`** — the generic subagent dispatcher (one wrapper handles every subagent — see "Subagents" below).
25	
26	## Subagents
27	
28	Subagents are descriptor files at `.archon/subagents/<name>.md` (YAML frontmatter — `name`, `description`, `write_domain`, `read_only`, `can_spawn`, `default_enabled`, optional `mandatory: [<phase>...]`, optional `dispatcher_notes` — followed by the prompt body the spawned agent reads).
29	
30	**You do NOT need to discover subagents.** The plan and review prompts auto-inject an **Available subagents** section at the top of each invocation listing every enabled descriptor with description, write-domain, MANDATORY flag, and `dispatcher_notes`. When you decide to invoke a subagent, read its full prompt at `.archon/subagents/<name>.md`.
31	
32	**Invoke** via the generic wrapper (Bash tool, foreground):
33	
34	```
35	python3 .claude/tools/archon-subagent.py \
36	  --name <subagent-name> \
37	  --slug <kebab-slug> \
38	  --directive-file <path-to-directive.md> \
39	  --write-domain '<glob>'        # repeat for multiple
40	```
41	
42	The wrapper exits 0 on success, prints a one-line status, writes the report to `.archon/task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`, and the Archon CLI auto-archives a copy to `logs/iter-NNN/<name>-<slug>-report.md` for the dashboard. The dispatch semaphore caps concurrent subagent processes at `loop.max_parallel`; the wrapper resolves `--parent-slug` from `ARCHON_SUBAGENT_SLUG` so nested children inherit the hierarchy.
43	
44	**A dispatch is a BLOCKING Bash call — wait by staying inside it.** `archon-subagent.py` is synchronous (it `subprocess.run`s the `archon subagent` CLI and returns only once the child finishes and its report is on disk). A long dispatch may be auto-backgrounded with a task ID — that's fine; wait for that task's result before acting. **Do NOT use the `Monitor` tool to wait** — it is non-blocking (returns immediately, "keep working"), so your turn ends and the subagents are abandoned mid-run. **Do NOT use foreground `sleep` / `until … ; do sleep … ; done`** — the harness blocks `sleep`. To run independent subagents **in parallel**, put all their dispatches in **one Bash call**, each backgrounded with `&`, ending with `wait` — e.g. `python3 .claude/tools/archon-subagent.py --name A … & python3 .claude/tools/archon-subagent.py --name B … & wait` — which blocks until all finish (the whole wave as one blocking dispatch). Serialize only subagents that write the **same file** (separate sequential calls). The rule never changes: do not consume a subagent's output until its report exists.
45	
46	**Highly-recommended subagents.** A descriptor whose frontmatter sets `mandatory: [plan]` is treated as **highly recommended** for the plan phase (similarly `[review]` for the review phase). The catalog tags them `[HIGHLY RECOMMENDED]`. Dispatch each one unless you have a concrete reason to skip — when skipping, record a one-line rationale under a `## Subagent skips` section in `iter/iter-NNN/<phase>.md` (e.g. `- strategy-critic: STRATEGY.md SHA unchanged from prior iter and last verdict was SOUND`). A post-phase audit checks both signals: it silences when the subagent dispatched OR a skip rationale was recorded, and warns only when neither happened. Each subagent's `dispatcher_notes` enumerates its specific skip conditions — read them before deciding. **Subagents ship disabled by default** (`default_enabled: false`), so the rule only fires for subagents the user has explicitly listed under `subagents.enabled` in `.archon/config.json`; on a fresh project the catalog is empty and no recommended dispatch is required.
47	
48	## Key Files & Permissions
49	
50	All Archon state files are in `.archon/`. The table below covers **phase agents** (plan, prover, review) and the **user**. Subagent permissions are NOT a fixed row — they are descriptor-driven (`write_domain` glob in the subagent's frontmatter, enforced at dispatch time). See "Subagent permissions" after the table.
51	
52	| File | Plan | Prover | Review | User |
53	|------|------|--------|--------|------|
54	| `PROGRESS.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
55	| `STRATEGY.md` | read + write | — | — | read |
56	| `ARCHON_MEMORY.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read (edits via `archon discuss`) |
57	| `USER_HINTS.md` | **loop-managed** (captured + cleared by the CLI; injected into the plan prompt) | — | — | write |
58	| `task_pending.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
59	| `task_done.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
60	| `task_results/<file>.md` | read (collect) | write (own file) | read only | read |
61	| `proof-journal/` | read | — | **write** | read |
62	| `PROJECT_STATUS.md` | read | — | **write** (Knowledge Base only — session log lives in `iter/iter-NNN/review.md`) | read |
63	| `iter/iter-NNN/plan.md` | **write** (this iter only) | — | read (recent window injected) | read |
64	| `iter/iter-NNN/review.md` | read (recent window injected) | — | **write** (this iter only) | read |
65	| `iter/iter-NNN/objectives.md` | optional write | — | read | read |
66	| `archon-protected.yaml` | read | read | read | **write** |
67	| `.lean` files | — | write (own file; frozen protected signatures) | — | write (via comments) |
68	| `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` | **write** (informal prose, `\lean{...}` hints) | read | **write** (semantic markers: `\mathlibok`, `% NOTE:`, `\lean{...}` corrections — NOT `\leanok`) | read |
69	| `analogies/<slug>.md` | read (when relevant) | — | — | read |
70	| `TO_USER.md` | **write** (shared notice board) | **write** (shared notice board) | **write** (shared notice board) | read |
71	
72	### Subagent permissions
73	
74	Each subagent ships YAML frontmatter:
75	
76	- `write_domain: "<glob>"` — file pattern the subagent may write. Empty for purely read-only subagents (the dispatching call typically passes `--write-domain 'task_results/**'` so the subagent can still write its own report).
77	- `read_only: true | false` — when `true`, the subagent may only write its `task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`.
78	- `can_spawn: true | false` — when `true`, the subagent may dispatch children whose `write_domain` is a subset of its own.
79	
80	The dispatcher enforces `write_domain` at the filesystem level: any child whose declared domain is not a subset of the parent's is rejected before the child agent starts. Reads are restricted only by the descriptor's prompt body (e.g. fresh-context critics that are told not to read PROGRESS.md / STRATEGY.md).
81	
82	**`.archon/REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md`** is reserved for the interactive `archon refactor draft` / `archon refactor run` flow run by hand by the mathematician. The autonomous loop never reads or writes that file. To invoke a refactor-style subagent inside the loop, write the directive to a fresh tempfile under `.archon/logs/iter-NNN/<name>-<slug>-directive.md` and dispatch via the generic wrapper.
83	
84	## Protected Declarations
85	
86	`archon-protected.yaml` at the project root lists declarations whose **signatures are frozen** by the mathematician. Rules:
87	
88	- **Plan / prover / review** agents: read-only on protected signatures. May fill proof bodies; may not rename, re-type, or reorder arguments.
89	- **Write-capable structural subagents** (per their `write_domain`): may *move* a protected declaration to a different file (name + signature preserved verbatim) and must then update the path key in `archon-protected.yaml`. They may NEVER rename / re-type / delete / re-sign a protected declaration.
90	- The YAML file itself is user-only. No agent adds or removes a declaration. The only allowed agent edit is updating an existing declaration's file path.
91	
92	## Blueprint Marker Vocabulary
93	
94	Two semantic markers:
95	
96	- `\leanok` — statement block: declaration is formalized (at least a `sorry` present). Proof block: proof closed, no `sorry`.
97	- `\mathlibok` — statement block: declaration is backed by Mathlib (re-export / alias); no Archon proof obligation remains.
98	
99	No marker means the block is unformalized. If a block fails to translate, leave it unmarked and annotate with a `% NOTE:` comment.
100	
101	**`\leanok` is managed deterministically by the `sync_leanok` phase** that runs between prover and review each iteration. It walks every chapter, looks up each `\lean{...}` declaration, runs `sorry_analyzer` + `lake build <module>` (it uses `lake build`, not `lake env lean <file>`, so dependencies are built and missing-import failures aren't spurious), and adds/removes `\leanok` accordingly. Agents should NOT add or remove `\leanok` as routine bookkeeping — leave it to the sync. The one exception is the review agent: if it is **certain** the sync got a marker wrong (a clearly-finished proof left unmarked, or a marker present on something incomplete), it may manually correct it, but must explicitly surface and justify that override in its summary (see `.archon/prompts/review.md`).
102	
103	`\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, and `% NOTE:` annotations are the review agent's domain (they require semantic judgement). The plan agent writes informal prose and `\lean{...}` hints; provers never touch the blueprint.
104	
105	## User Interaction
106	
107	The user provides hints in two places:
108	
109	- **Strategic hints** → `.archon/USER_HINTS.md`. The Archon loop captures this file's content before each plan phase, injects it into the plan agent's prompt, and resets the file to its bundled template (an HTML-comment format guide + zero bullets) after the plan phase succeeds. The HTML-comment preamble is stripped before injection so a template-only file renders as "no hints" to the planner. The plan agent does NOT read or clear that file itself. Provers never see these hints.
110	- **File-specific hints** → `/- USER: ... -/` comments directly in `.lean` files. The prover that owns that file sees them naturally.
111	
112	Archon talks back to the user through **`TO_USER.md`** — a single, *persistent* notice board surfaced as a UI banner. It is **shared and writable by every agent** (plan, prover, review). It is no longer cleared at the start of each review phase; content survives across iters until an agent prunes it. The discipline every writer follows:
113	
114	- **Concise**: the whole file stays ≤ 2–3 short bullets. It is a banner, not a log.
115	- **Relevant now**: before adding anything, read the current content and **delete any bullet that is no longer true or important at this point** (the blocker was resolved, the decision was overtaken, the credential got set). Never let it accumulate stale notices.
116	- **Notice board, never a question queue**: the loop is autonomous and may run unattended for many iters. Never write "awaiting your decision", an options menu, a "where to reply", or "default to X if no reply" — anything implying the loop is paused for a human. State decisions as *made* and blockers as *worked-around-as-far-as-possible*; the user steers asynchronously via `USER_HINTS.md`.
117	
118	## Agent Roles
119	
120	### Plan Agent
121	
122	- Read `.archon/prompts/plan.md` for full instructions.
123	- User hints, blueprint-doctor findings, recent iter sidecars, the subagent catalog, and the references summary are all pre-injected into your invocation prompt. You do not "go read" those files.
124	- Read `task_results/` to collect prover and subagent results, then update `task_pending.md` / `task_done.md`.
125	- Optionally invoke subagents (via the auto-injected catalog) before writing prover objectives. Subagent reports are auto-archived to `logs/iter-NNN/` by the Archon CLI.
126	- Write `PROGRESS.md` with objectives for the next prover round.
127	- Curate `ARCHON_MEMORY.md` — the condensed, cross-iteration knowledge file injected (read-only) into every agent's prompt. Keep only durable facts that would surprise an agent reading the code fresh (dead-end tactics, files not to touch, Mathlib gap coordinates, protected invariants). Hard limits: ~10 bullets / ~600 chars; prune before adding. Do NOT duplicate what's obvious from the code or PROGRESS.md.
128	- Write informal prose in `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex`. Do NOT touch markers — `\leanok` is owned by the deterministic sync; `\mathlibok` / `% NOTE:` by the review agent.
129	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or fill sorries yourself.
130	
131	### Prover Agent
132	
133	- Read `PROGRESS.md` for your objectives (read only).
134	- Your **active mode** is injected directly into your invocation prompt — read it carefully, it defines your goal, workflow, and constraints for this session.
135	- When your mode specifies `read_blueprint: true`, you are permitted to **read** your assigned blueprint chapter (`blueprint/src/chapters/<your_slug>.tex`) for the informal proof sketch. You may never write to blueprint chapters.
136	- Write results to `task_results/<your_file>.md`.
137	- A prover lane ends by writing its task result and exiting normally. **Do not kill Lean, Lake, shell, or sibling agent processes from inside the prover lane.** Do not run `pkill`, `killall`, regex-based process cleanup, or background-verification cleanup. If a verification command is still running, wait for its result or report that it is still running; process lifetime is owned by the Archon runner/orchestrator.
138	- Write only to the `.lean` file(s) you are assigned — never edit another agent's file.
139	- Check for `/- USER: ... -/` comments in your file for file-specific hints.
140	- Do NOT edit blueprint chapters. Flag in your task result which declarations are ready for which marker; the review agent applies them.
141	
142	### Subagents
143	
144	Subagents are descriptor-driven and discovered at runtime. The catalog is auto-injected into the plan and review prompts each phase (see "Subagents" above for the wrapper invocation). Each subagent reads only what its directive points at — never `PROGRESS.md` / `STRATEGY.md` / phase-agent state — and writes a self-contained report at `task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`. Descriptors with `can_spawn: true` may dispatch children (subject to the global `loop.max_parallel` cap).
145	
146	### Review Agent
147	
148	- Read `.archon/prompts/review.md` for full instructions.
149	- Read `proof-journal/current_session/attempts_raw.jsonl` for structured prover attempt data.
150	- Write the session journal to `proof-journal/sessions/session_N/` (`summary.md`, `milestones.jsonl`, `recommendations.md`).
151	- Update `PROJECT_STATUS.md` (Knowledge Base only — session narrative goes to `iter/iter-NNN/review.md`).
152	- Maintain the semantic blueprint markers — `\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, `% NOTE:` annotations, stale `\notready` removal. Normally leave `\leanok` to the deterministic `sync_leanok` phase; only override a marker the sync got demonstrably wrong, and justify it in your summary.
153	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or modify `PROGRESS.md`.
154	
155	### Loop infrastructure (no agent role)
156	
157	These run automatically between phases:
158	
159	- **Iter sidecar init** — creates `.archon/iter/iter-NNN/` at iter start so plan + review have a stable destination for per-iter narrative. Top-level files (STRATEGY.md, PROJECT_STATUS.md, task_*.md) stay bounded.
160	- **`sync_leanok`** — runs between prover and review, deterministically updating `\leanok` markers based on actual sorry counts and compilation status.
161	- **`blueprint-doctor`** — runs between prover and review, deterministically detecting orphan chapters, broken `\ref` / `\uses` / `\proves`, and new `axiom` declarations. Findings are written to `logs/iter-NNN/blueprint-doctor.{md,json}` and inlined into the next plan agent's prompt.

59	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — abstract wide-fibre-power ↔ slice-product
60	
61	These `CategoryTheory`-namespace declarations are the abstract categorical core of the Stub-1
62	geometric backbone decomposition (`cechBackbone_left_sigma`).  They are stated for an arbitrary

67	namespace CategoryTheory
68	
69	/-- The wide pullback over `S` of a family `(g k : Z k ⟶ S)`, viewed in `Over S`, is a limit fan of
70	the legs `Over.mk (g k)`: i.e. the wide fibre power over `S` is the product of the legs in the slice
71	`Over S`.  Project-local: Mathlib has `WidePullbackCone.isLimitOfFan` (wide pullback over a terminal
72	base = product of legs) but not this direct slice-product identification of the fibre power. -/
73	noncomputable def widePullback_overX_isLimit {C : Type*} [Category C] {S : C}
74	    {ι : Type*} {Z : ι → C} (g : (k : ι) → Z k ⟶ S) [HasWidePullback S Z g] :
75	    IsLimit (Fan.mk (C := Over S) (f := fun k => Over.mk (g k))
76	      (Over.mk (WidePullback.base g))
77	      (fun k => Over.homMk (WidePullback.π g k) (WidePullback.π_arrow g k))) :=
78	  mkFanLimit _
79	    (fun s => Over.homMk
80	      (WidePullback.lift s.pt.hom (fun k => (s.proj k).left) (fun k => Over.w (s.proj k)))
81	      (WidePullback.lift_base _ _ _ _))
82	    (fun s k => by
83	      apply Over.OverMorphism.ext
84	      change (WidePullback.lift _ _ _ ≫ WidePullback.π g k) = (s.proj k).left
85	      exact WidePullback.lift_π _ _ _ _ _)
86	    (fun s m hm => by
87	      apply Over.OverMorphism.ext
88	      change m.left = WidePullback.lift _ _ _
89	      apply WidePullback.hom_ext
90	      · intro k
91	        rw [WidePullback.lift_π]
92	        exact congrArg CommaMorphism.left (hm k)
93	      · rw [WidePullback.lift_base]
94	        exact Over.w m)
95	
96	/-- The wide fibre power over `S` is the iterated product in the slice: in `Over S` the object
97	`Over.mk (WidePullback.base g)` carrying the wide pullback over `S` is the product
98	`∏ᶜ fun k => Over.mk (g k)` of the legs.  Project-local foundational step of the Stub-1 backbone
99	decomposition (blueprint `lem:widePullback_overX_eq_prod`). -/
100	noncomputable def widePullback_overX_eq_prod {C : Type*} [Category C] {S : C}

101	    {ι : Type*} {Z : ι → C} (g : (k : ι) → Z k ⟶ S) [HasWidePullback S Z g]
102	    [HasProduct (fun k => Over.mk (g k))] :
103	    Over.mk (WidePullback.base g) ≅ ∏ᶜ fun k => Over.mk (g k) :=
104	  (widePullback_overX_isLimit g).conePointUniqueUpToIso (productIsProduct _)

106	/-- The cofan exhibiting `Over.mk (Sigma.desc f)` as the coproduct of the legs `Over.mk (f i)` in
107	`Over S`.  Abstract version of `AlgebraicGeometry.coverArrowOverCofan`. -/
108	noncomputable def overSigmaDescCofan {C : Type*} [Category C] {S : C} {ι : Type*}
109	    {Z : ι → C} (f : (i : ι) → Z i ⟶ S) [HasCoproduct Z] :
110	    Cofan (fun i => Over.mk (f i)) :=
111	  Cofan.mk (Over.mk (Limits.Sigma.desc f))
112	    (fun i => Over.homMk (Limits.Sigma.ι Z i) (by simp [Limits.Sigma.ι_desc]))
113	
114	/-- `overSigmaDescCofan` is a colimit: in `Over S` the object `Over.mk (Sigma.desc f)` is the
115	coproduct of the legs `Over.mk (f i)`.  Abstract version of
116	`AlgebraicGeometry.coverArrowOverIsColimit`. -/
117	noncomputable def overSigmaDescIsColimit {C : Type*} [Category C] {S : C} {ι : Type*}
118	    {Z : ι → C} (f : (i : ι) → Z i ⟶ S) [HasCoproduct Z] :
119	    IsColimit (overSigmaDescCofan f) := by
120	  haveI : HasCoproduct (fun i => (Over.mk (f i)).left) := (inferInstanceAs (HasCoproduct Z))
121	  refine mkCofanColimit _
122	    (fun t => Over.homMk (Limits.Sigma.desc (fun i => (t.inj i).left)) ?_)
123	    (fun t j => ?_) (fun t m hm => ?_)
124	  · change Limits.Sigma.desc (fun i => (t.inj i).left) ≫ t.pt.hom = Limits.Sigma.desc f
125	    refine Limits.Sigma.hom_ext _ _ (fun i => ?_)
126	    rw [Limits.Sigma.ι_desc_assoc, Over.w]
127	    exact (Limits.Sigma.ι_desc f i).symm
128	  · apply Over.OverMorphism.ext
129	    simp [overSigmaDescCofan, Limits.Sigma.ι_desc]
130	  · apply Over.OverMorphism.ext
131	    refine Limits.Sigma.hom_ext _ _ (fun i => ?_)
132	    have h := congrArg CommaMorphism.left (hm i)
133	    simp only [overSigmaDescCofan, Cofan.inj, Cofan.mk,
134	      Discrete.natTrans_app, Over.homMk_left, Limits.Sigma.ι_desc] at h ⊢
135	    exact h

137	/-- In `Over S`, the object `Over.mk (Sigma.desc f)` carrying the descent map is the coproduct of
138	the legs `Over.mk (f i)`.  Abstract version of `AlgebraicGeometry.coverArrowOverSigmaIso`; used to
139	rewrite the inner `∐ᵢ Z i` of the Stub-1 fibre power inside the slice. -/
140	noncomputable def overSigmaDescIso {C : Type*} [Category C] {S : C} {ι : Type*}
141	    {Z : ι → C} (f : (i : ι) → Z i ⟶ S) [HasCoproduct Z]
142	    [HasCoproduct (fun i => Over.mk (f i))] :
143	    (∐ fun i => Over.mk (f i)) ≅ Over.mk (Limits.Sigma.desc f) :=
144	  (coproductIsCoproduct _).coconePointUniqueUpToIso (overSigmaDescIsColimit f)

146	namespace FinitaryPreExtensive

148	/-- Splitting a finite product off its head: `∏ᶜ X ≅ X 0 ⨯ ∏ᶜ (fun i => X i.succ)` for
149	`X : Fin (n+1) → C`.  Built directly as a limit fan (`mkFanLimit`) — Mathlib has no `Fin`-succ
150	categorical product split.  This is the recursion that lets the wide fibre power
151	`∏ᶜ (Fin(p+2) copies)` be peeled into `head ×_S ∏ᶜ (Fin(p+1) copies)` in the slice during the
152	induction of `widePullback_coproduct_iso`.  Project-local. -/
153	noncomputable def prodFinSuccIso {C : Type*} [Category C] [HasFiniteProducts C] {n : ℕ}
154	    (X : Fin (n + 1) → C) :
155	    (∏ᶜ X) ≅ X 0 ⨯ (∏ᶜ fun i : Fin n => X i.succ) := by
156	  refine (productIsProduct X).conePointUniqueUpToIso (mkFanLimit
157	    (Fan.mk (X 0 ⨯ (∏ᶜ fun i : Fin n => X i.succ))
158	      (fun j => Fin.cases prod.fst (fun i => prod.snd ≫ Pi.π (fun i : Fin n => X i.succ) i) j))
159	    (fun s => prod.lift (s.proj 0) (Pi.lift (fun i : Fin n => s.proj i.succ)))
160	    (fun s j => ?_) (fun s m hm => ?_))
161	  · refine Fin.cases ?_ ?_ j
162	    · change prod.lift (s.proj 0) _ ≫ prod.fst = s.proj 0
163	      rw [prod.lift_fst]
164	    · intro i
165	      change prod.lift _ _ ≫ (prod.snd ≫ Pi.π (fun i : Fin n => X i.succ) i) = s.proj i.succ
166	      rw [← Category.assoc, prod.lift_snd, Pi.lift_π]
167	  · apply prod.hom_ext
168	    · rw [prod.lift_fst]
169	      have := hm 0; change m ≫ prod.fst = s.proj 0 at this; exact this
170	    · rw [prod.lift_snd]
171	      apply Pi.hom_ext; intro i
172	      rw [Pi.lift_π]
173	      have := hm i.succ
174	      change m ≫ (prod.snd ≫ Pi.π (fun i : Fin n => X i.succ) i) = s.proj i.succ at this
175	      rw [← Category.assoc] at this; exact this

177	/-- One-sided distributivity of the fibre product over a coproduct in `C`:
178	`∐ᵢ (A ×_S Y i) ≅ A ×_S (∐ᵢ Y i)`.  Derived from `isIso_sigmaDesc_fst` (universality of finite
179	coproducts in a finitary pre-extensive category) by pulling the coproduct injections back along the
180	second projection of `A ×_S ∐Y` (pasting via `pullbackLeftPullbackSndIso`).  Blueprint
181	`lem:prod_coproduct_distrib` (the `C`-level fibre-product form of the one-sided distributivity used
182	in the induction of `widePullback_coproduct_iso`). -/
183	noncomputable def prod_coproduct_distrib {C : Type*} [Category C] [HasPullbacks C]
184	    [FinitaryPreExtensive C] {ι : Type} [Finite ι] {S : C} (A : C) (a : A ⟶ S)
185	    {Y : ι → C} (g : (i : ι) → Y i ⟶ S) :
186	    (∐ fun i => pullback a (g i)) ≅ pullback a (Limits.Sigma.desc g) := by
187	  have hπ : IsIso (Limits.Sigma.desc (Limits.Sigma.ι Y)) := by
188	    rw [show Limits.Sigma.desc (Limits.Sigma.ι Y) = 𝟙 _ from
189	      Limits.Sigma.hom_ext _ _ (fun i => by rw [Limits.Sigma.ι_desc, Category.comp_id])]
190	    infer_instance
191	  have key := FinitaryPreExtensive.isIso_sigmaDesc_fst (Limits.Sigma.ι Y)
192	    (pullback.snd a (Limits.Sigma.desc g)) hπ
193	  let e : (i : ι) → pullback (pullback.snd a (Limits.Sigma.desc g)) (Limits.Sigma.ι Y i)
194	      ≅ pullback a (g i) := fun i =>
195	    pullbackLeftPullbackSndIso a (Limits.Sigma.desc g) (Limits.Sigma.ι Y i) ≪≫
196	      pullback.congrHom rfl (by rw [Limits.Sigma.ι_desc])
197	  exact (Limits.Sigma.mapIso e).symm ≪≫ asIso (Limits.Sigma.desc
198	    (fun i => pullback.fst (pullback.snd a (Limits.Sigma.desc g)) (Limits.Sigma.ι Y i)))
199	
200	/-- Nested-coproduct flatten + `Fin.cons` reindex: `∐ᵢ ∐_τ F(cons i τ) ≅ ∐_σ F σ` over the
201	`(p+2)`-fold multi-indices.  Pure reindexing (`sigmaSigmaIso` collapses the nested coproduct; the
202	`Fin.consEquiv` reindexes the resulting pairs `(i, τ) ↦ Fin.cons i τ`).  Blueprint
203	`lem:coproduct_fibrePower_reindex`.  The identification of the component `F (Fin.cons i τ)` with the
204	`(p+2)`-fold fibre power lives in the inductive step that consumes this lemma. -/
205	noncomputable def coproduct_fibrePower_reindex {C : Type*} [Category C] {ι : Type} [Finite ι]
206	    [HasFiniteCoproducts C] (p : ℕ) (F : (Fin (p + 2) → ι) → C) :
207	    (∐ fun i : ι => ∐ fun τ : Fin (p + 1) → ι => F (Fin.cons i τ))
208	      ≅ ∐ fun σ : Fin (p + 2) → ι => F σ :=
209	  sigmaSigmaIso (fun _ : ι => (Fin (p + 1) → ι)) (fun i τ => F (Fin.cons i τ)) ≪≫
210	  Sigma.whiskerEquiv
211	    ((Equiv.sigmaEquivProd ι (Fin (p + 1) → ι)).trans (Fin.consEquiv (fun _ => ι)))
212	    (fun _ => Iso.refl _)
213	
214	/-- Base case of the wide-fibre-power decomposition (`p = 0`): the `1`-fold wide fibre power of the
215	descent map `∐ᵢ Z i ⟶ S` over `S` is, in `Over S`, the coproduct over `σ : Fin 1 → ι` of the
216	`1`-fold fibre powers (here written as products in the slice).  Pure reindexing — no extensivity
217	needed — chaining `widePullback_overX_eq_prod`, `productUniqueIso` (over `Fin 1`), `overSigmaDescIso`
218	and the coproduct reindex along `(Fin 1 → ι) ≃ ι`.  Blueprint `lem:coproduct_distrib_fibrePower_zero`.
219	
220	Note: the σ-component is the slice product `∏ᶜ fun k => Over.mk (f (σ k))`, which is identified with
221	the wide fibre power `Over.mk (WidePullback.base (fun k => f (σ k)))` via `widePullback_overX_eq_prod`
222	at the assembly site.  This is the project's chosen (slice-product) normal form for the components,
223	which minimizes the `HasWidePullback` instance bookkeeping in the induction. -/
224	noncomputable def widePullback_coproduct_iso_zero {C : Type*} [Category C] [HasPullbacks C]
225	    {S : C} {ι : Type*} [Finite ι] {Z : ι → C} (f : (i : ι) → Z i ⟶ S)
226	    [HasFiniteWidePullbacks C] [HasFiniteCoproducts C]
227	    [HasFiniteProducts (Over S)] [HasFiniteCoproducts (Over S)] :
228	    Over.mk (WidePullback.base (fun _ : Fin 1 => Limits.Sigma.desc f))
229	      ≅ ∐ (fun σ : Fin 1 → ι => ∏ᶜ (fun k : Fin 1 => Over.mk (f (σ k)))) :=
230	  widePullback_overX_eq_prod (fun _ : Fin 1 => Limits.Sigma.desc f) ≪≫
231	  productUniqueIso (fun _ : Fin 1 => Over.mk (Limits.Sigma.desc f)) ≪≫
232	  (overSigmaDescIso f).symm ≪≫
233	  Sigma.whiskerEquiv (Equiv.funUnique (Fin 1) ι).symm
234	    (fun i => productUniqueIso
235	      (fun k : Fin 1 => Over.mk (f (((Equiv.funUnique (Fin 1) ι).symm i) k))))
236

238	/-- One-sided distributivity in `Over S`'s underlying category, coproduct in the FIRST pullback
239	argument: `∐ᵢ pullback (gᵢ) b ≅ pullback (Sigma.desc g) b`.  Derived from the project's
240	`prod_coproduct_distrib` (coproduct-second form) by conjugating with `pullbackSymmetry`.
241	Project-local: consumed by `overProd_coproduct_distrib`'s structure-map compatibility. -/
242	noncomputable def coprodFirst_distrib {C : Type*} [Category C] [HasPullbacks C] [FinitaryPreExtensive C] {ι : Type} [Finite ι] {S : C}
243	    (B : C) (b : B ⟶ S) {Y : ι → C} (g : (i : ι) → Y i ⟶ S) :
244	    (∐ fun i => pullback (g i) b) ≅ pullback (Limits.Sigma.desc g) b :=
245	  asIso (Limits.Sigma.map (fun i => (pullbackSymmetry (g i) b).hom)) ≪≫
246	    prod_coproduct_distrib B b g ≪≫ pullbackSymmetry b (Limits.Sigma.desc g)

249	/-- `prod_coproduct_distrib` is compatible with the first projection to the base of `a`:
250	its hom followed by `pullback.fst` is the descent of the per-summand `pullback.fst`. Project-local
251	compatibility lemma used to verify the structure-map condition of `overProd_coproduct_distrib`. -/
252	lemma pcd_hom_fst {C : Type*} [Category C] [HasPullbacks C] [FinitaryPreExtensive C] {ι : Type} [Finite ι] {S : C} (A : C) (a : A ⟶ S) {Y : ι → C} (g : (i : ι) → Y i ⟶ S) :
253	    (prod_coproduct_distrib A a g).hom ≫ pullback.fst a (Limits.Sigma.desc g)
254	      = Limits.Sigma.desc (fun i => pullback.fst a (g i)) := by
255	  refine Limits.Sigma.hom_ext _ _ (fun i => ?_)
256	  rw [Limits.Sigma.ι_desc]
257	  have hstep : (prod_coproduct_distrib A a g).hom
258	      = (Limits.Sigma.map (fun i => (pullbackLeftPullbackSndIso a (Limits.Sigma.desc g) (Sigma.ι Y i) ≪≫
259	            pullback.congrHom rfl (by rw [Limits.Sigma.ι_desc])).inv)) ≫
260	          Limits.Sigma.desc (fun i => pullback.fst (pullback.snd a (Limits.Sigma.desc g)) (Sigma.ι Y i)) := by
261	    simp only [prod_coproduct_distrib, Iso.trans_hom, Iso.symm_hom, asIso_hom]
262	    congr 1
263	  rw [hstep]
264	  simp only [Category.assoc, Limits.Sigma.ι_map_assoc, Limits.Sigma.ι_desc_assoc]
265	  simp
266	  simp only [pullback.map]
267	  rw [pullback.lift_fst]
268	  simp

270	/-- `prod_coproduct_distrib` compatibility with the second projection (the coproduct side):
271	its hom followed by `pullback.snd` descends to the per-summand `pullback.snd ≫ Sigma.ι`.
272	Project-local compatibility lemma for `overProd_coproduct_distrib`. -/
273	lemma pcd_hom_snd {C : Type*} [Category C] [HasPullbacks C] [FinitaryPreExtensive C] {ι : Type} [Finite ι] {S : C} (A : C) (a : A ⟶ S) {Y : ι → C} (g : (i : ι) → Y i ⟶ S) :
274	    (prod_coproduct_distrib A a g).hom ≫ pullback.snd a (Limits.Sigma.desc g)
275	      = Limits.Sigma.desc (fun i => pullback.snd a (g i) ≫ Sigma.ι Y i) := by
276	  refine Limits.Sigma.hom_ext _ _ (fun i => ?_)
277	  rw [Limits.Sigma.ι_desc]
278	  have hstep : (prod_coproduct_distrib A a g).hom
279	      = (Limits.Sigma.map (fun i => (pullbackLeftPullbackSndIso a (Limits.Sigma.desc g) (Sigma.ι Y i) ≪≫
280	            pullback.congrHom rfl (by rw [Limits.Sigma.ι_desc])).inv)) ≫
281	          Limits.Sigma.desc (fun i => pullback.fst (pullback.snd a (Limits.Sigma.desc g)) (Sigma.ι Y i)) := by
282	    simp only [prod_coproduct_distrib, Iso.trans_hom, Iso.symm_hom, asIso_hom]
283	    congr 1
284	  rw [hstep]
285	  simp only [Category.assoc, Limits.Sigma.ι_map_assoc, Limits.Sigma.ι_desc_assoc]
286	  rw [pullback.condition]
287	  simp
288	  simp only [pullback.map]
289	  rw [pullback.lift_snd_assoc]
290	  simp

292	/-- `coprodFirst_distrib` compatibility with `pullback.fst` (the coproduct side): descends to the
293	per-summand `pullback.fst ≫ Sigma.ι`.  Project-local, used in `overProd_coproduct_distrib`. -/
294	lemma cf_hom_fst {C : Type*} [Category C] [HasPullbacks C] [FinitaryPreExtensive C] {ι : Type} [Finite ι] {S : C} (B : C) (b : B ⟶ S) {Y : ι → C} (g : (i : ι) → Y i ⟶ S) :
295	    (coprodFirst_distrib B b g).hom ≫ pullback.fst (Limits.Sigma.desc g) b
296	      = Limits.Sigma.desc (fun i => pullback.fst (g i) b ≫ Sigma.ι Y i) := by
297	  rw [coprodFirst_distrib]
298	  simp only [Iso.trans_hom, asIso_hom, Category.assoc]
299	  rw [pullbackSymmetry_hom_comp_fst, pcd_hom_snd]
300	  refine Limits.Sigma.hom_ext _ _ (fun j => ?_)
301	  rw [← Category.assoc, Limits.Sigma.ι_map, Category.assoc, Limits.Sigma.ι_desc,
302	    ← Category.assoc, pullbackSymmetry_hom_comp_snd, Limits.Sigma.ι_desc]

304	private lemma overSigma_hom_eq {C : Type*} [Category C] [HasPullbacks C] {S : C} {ι : Type} [Finite ι] [HasFiniteCoproducts C]
305	    (A : ι → Over S) :
306	    (∐ A).hom = (PreservesCoproduct.iso (Over.forget S) A).hom ≫
307	      Limits.Sigma.desc (fun i => (A i).hom) := by
308	  haveI : HasColimit (Discrete.functor A ⋙ Over.forget S) :=
309	    hasColimit_of_iso (F := Discrete.functor (fun i => (A i).left))
310	      (Discrete.natIso (fun i => Iso.refl _))
311	  refine (PreservesCoproduct.iso (Over.forget S) A).inv_comp_eq.mp ?_
312	  rw [PreservesCoproduct.inv_hom]
313	  refine Limits.Sigma.hom_ext _ _ (fun i => ?_)
314	  rw [ι_comp_sigmaComparison_assoc]
315	  show (Sigma.ι A i).left ≫ (∐ A).hom = _
316	  rw [Limits.Sigma.ι_desc]
317	  exact Over.w (Sigma.ι A i)

319	/-- One-sided distributivity of the binary product over a finite coproduct in the slice category
320	`Over S` of a finitary pre-extensive category: `(∐ᵢ Aᵢ) ⨯ B ≅ ∐ᵢ (Aᵢ ⨯ B)`.  Built via
321	`Over.isoMk` from the `C`-level `prod_coproduct_distrib`, threading `Over.prodLeftIsoPullback`
322	and the coproduct-preservation of `Over.forget`.  This is the slice-product distributivity the
323	inductive step of `widePullback_coproduct_iso` consumes (blueprint `lem:overProd_coproduct_distrib`). -/
324	noncomputable def overProd_coproduct_distrib {C : Type*} [Category C] [HasPullbacks C] [FinitaryPreExtensive C] {ι : Type} [Finite ι] [HasFiniteCoproducts C]
325	    {S : C} [HasBinaryProducts (Over S)] (A : ι → Over S) (B : Over S) :
326	    (∐ A) ⨯ B ≅ ∐ fun i => A i ⨯ B := by
327	  set pA := PreservesCoproduct.iso (Over.forget S) A with hpA
328	  set pAB := PreservesCoproduct.iso (Over.forget S) (fun i => A i ⨯ B) with hpAB
329	  have hA : (∐ A).hom = pA.hom ≫ Limits.Sigma.desc (fun i => (A i).hom) := overSigma_hom_eq A
330	  have hAB : (∐ fun i => A i ⨯ B).hom
331	      = pAB.hom ≫ Limits.Sigma.desc (fun i => (A i ⨯ B).hom) := overSigma_hom_eq (fun i => A i ⨯ B)
332	  clear_value pA pAB
333	  have hAB' : pAB.inv ≫ (∐ fun i => A i ⨯ B).hom
334	      = Limits.Sigma.desc (fun i => (A i ⨯ B).hom) := by
335	    rw [hAB]; simp
336	  have hcond : (∐ A).hom ≫ 𝟙 S = pA.hom ≫ Limits.Sigma.desc (fun i => (A i).hom) := by
337	    rw [Category.comp_id]; exact hA
338	  refine Over.isoMk (Over.prodLeftIsoPullback (∐ A) B ≪≫
339	    asIso (pullback.map (∐ A).hom B.hom (Limits.Sigma.desc (fun i => (A i).hom)) B.hom
340	      pA.hom (𝟙 B.left) (𝟙 S) hcond (by simp)) ≪≫
341	    (coprodFirst_distrib B.left B.hom (fun i => (A i).hom)).symm ≪≫
342	    asIso (Limits.Sigma.map (fun i => (Over.prodLeftIsoPullback (A i) B).inv)) ≪≫
343	    pAB.symm) ?_
344	  show _ ≫ (∐ fun i => A i ⨯ B).hom = ((∐ A) ⨯ B).hom
345	  have hR : ((∐ A) ⨯ B).hom
346	      = (Over.prodLeftIsoPullback (∐ A) B).hom ≫ pullback.fst (∐ A).hom B.hom ≫ (∐ A).hom := by
347	    rw [← Over.w (prod.fst (X := ∐ A) (Y := B)), ← Over.prodLeftIsoPullback_hom_fst, Category.assoc]
348	  have e3eq : (coprodFirst_distrib B.left B.hom (fun i => (A i).hom)).inv ≫
349	      Limits.Sigma.desc (fun i => pullback.fst (A i).hom B.hom ≫ (A i).hom)
350	      = pullback.fst (Limits.Sigma.desc (fun i => (A i).hom)) B.hom ≫
351	          Limits.Sigma.desc (fun i => (A i).hom) := by
352	    rw [Iso.inv_comp_eq, ← Category.assoc, cf_hom_fst]
353	    refine Limits.Sigma.hom_ext _ _ (fun i => ?_)
354	    simp only [Limits.Sigma.ι_desc, Limits.Sigma.ι_desc_assoc, Category.assoc]
355	  have e4eq : Limits.Sigma.map (fun i => (Over.prodLeftIsoPullback (A i) B).inv) ≫
356	        Limits.Sigma.desc (fun i => (A i ⨯ B).hom)
357	        = Limits.Sigma.desc (fun i => pullback.fst (A i).hom B.hom ≫ (A i).hom) := by
358	    refine Limits.Sigma.hom_ext _ _ (fun i => ?_)
359	    rw [← Category.assoc, Limits.Sigma.ι_map, Category.assoc, Limits.Sigma.ι_desc,
360	      Limits.Sigma.ι_desc, ← Over.w (prod.fst (X := A i) (Y := B)),
361	      ← Over.prodLeftIsoPullback_inv_fst_assoc]
362	  rw [hR]
363	  simp only [Iso.trans_hom, asIso_hom, Category.assoc]
364	  simp only [Iso.symm_hom]
365	  erw [hAB']
366	  rw [e4eq, e3eq]
367	  congr 1
368	  rw [← Category.assoc]
369	  simp only [pullback.map]
370	  rw [pullback.lift_fst, Category.assoc]
371	  exact congrArg _ hA.symm

374	/-- Right-handed one-sided distributivity in `Over S`: `A ⨯ (∐ᵢ Yᵢ) ≅ ∐ᵢ (A ⨯ Yᵢ)`. -/
375	noncomputable def overProd_coproduct_distrib_right {C : Type*} [Category C] [HasPullbacks C]
376	    [FinitaryPreExtensive C] {ι : Type} [Finite ι] [HasFiniteCoproducts C]
377	    {S : C} [HasBinaryProducts (Over S)] (A : Over S) (Y : ι → Over S) :
378	    A ⨯ (∐ Y) ≅ ∐ fun i => A ⨯ Y i :=
379	  Limits.prod.braiding A (∐ Y) ≪≫ overProd_coproduct_distrib Y A ≪≫
380	    Limits.Sigma.mapIso (fun i => Limits.prod.braiding (Y i) A)
381	
382	-- The inductive step chains six iso layers (`widePullback_overX_eq_prod`, `prodFinSuccIso`,
383	-- two distributivity isos, the reindex), whose combined `whnf` over the nested fibre powers
384	-- exceeds the default heartbeat budget.
385	set_option maxHeartbeats 1600000 in
386	/-- Coproduct distributes over the `(p+1)`-fold wide fibre power of the cover map `∐ᵢ Zᵢ ⟶ S`,
387	in slice-product normal form (blueprint `lem:coproduct_distrib_fibrePower`). -/
388	noncomputable def widePullback_coproduct_iso {C : Type*} [Category C] [HasPullbacks C]
389	    [FinitaryPreExtensive C] {S : C} {ι : Type} [Finite ι] {Z : ι → C}
390	    (f : (i : ι) → Z i ⟶ S) [HasFiniteWidePullbacks C] [HasFiniteCoproducts C]
391	    [HasFiniteProducts (Over S)] [HasFiniteCoproducts (Over S)] :
392	    (p : ℕ) → (Over.mk (WidePullback.base (fun _ : Fin (p + 1) => Limits.Sigma.desc f))
393	      ≅ ∐ (fun σ : Fin (p + 1) → ι => ∏ᶜ (fun k : Fin (p + 1) => Over.mk (f (σ k)))))
394	  | 0 => widePullback_coproduct_iso_zero f
395	  | (p+1) => by
396	      refine widePullback_overX_eq_prod (fun _ : Fin (p + 2) => Limits.Sigma.desc f) ≪≫
397	        prodFinSuccIso (fun _ : Fin (p + 2) => Over.mk (Limits.Sigma.desc f)) ≪≫ ?_
398	      refine Limits.prod.mapIso (overSigmaDescIso f).symm
399	        ((widePullback_overX_eq_prod (fun _ : Fin (p + 1) => Limits.Sigma.desc f)).symm ≪≫
400	          widePullback_coproduct_iso f p) ≪≫ ?_
401	      refine overProd_coproduct_distrib (fun i => Over.mk (f i))
402	        (∐ fun τ : Fin (p + 1) → ι => ∏ᶜ fun k => Over.mk (f (τ k))) ≪≫ ?_
403	      refine Limits.Sigma.mapIso (fun i => overProd_coproduct_distrib_right (Over.mk (f i))
404	        (fun τ : Fin (p + 1) → ι => ∏ᶜ fun k => Over.mk (f (τ k)))) ≪≫ ?_
405	      have e7 := Limits.Sigma.mapIso (fun i => Limits.Sigma.mapIso (fun τ : Fin (p + 1) → ι =>
406	        (prodFinSuccIso (fun k : Fin (p + 2) => Over.mk (f (Fin.cons i τ k)))).symm))
407	      exact e7 ≪≫ coproduct_fibrePower_reindex p (fun σ => ∏ᶜ fun k => Over.mk (f (σ k)))
408

432	    · rw [Category.comp_id]

434	      intro a
435	      simp [Limits.coprod.inr_desc, Limits.Sigma.ι_desc_assoc, Limits.Sigma.ι_desc]

485	    rw [Scheme.Opens.range_ι]

488	    intro k

491	    rw [hcomp, ← SetLike.mem_coe, Scheme.Hom.coe_opensRange]

494	    (fun k => IsOpenImmersion.lift (g k) (⨅ k, (g k).opensRange).ι (by

705	scheme: the pair of restriction-to-component units. -/
706	private noncomputable def coprodDecompMap (M : (A ⨿ B).Modules) :
707	    M ⟶ (Scheme.Modules.pushforward (coprod.inl : A ⟶ A ⨿ B)).obj
708	          (M.restrict (coprod.inl : A ⟶ A ⨿ B)) ⨯
709	        (Scheme.Modules.pushforward (coprod.inr : B ⟶ A ⨿ B)).obj
710	          (M.restrict (coprod.inr : B ⟶ A ⨿ B)) :=
711	  prod.lift ((Scheme.Modules.restrictAdjunction (coprod.inl : A ⟶ A ⨿ B)).unit.app M)
712	    ((Scheme.Modules.restrictAdjunction (coprod.inr : B ⟶ A ⨿ B)).unit.app M)

716	are all proved axiom-clean below; the finite-index generalization `pushPull_coprod_prod` and
717	Stub 2 `pushPull_sigma_iso` chain them.  All three `coprodToProd_isIso_*` induction steps

722	a module sheaf on `A ⨿ B` is the product of its restrictions-to-component pushforwards.  This is the
723	binary disjoint-union sheaf decomposition (`TopCat.Sheaf.isProductOfDisjoint` on the underlying
724	abelian sheaf), reflected back to `(A ⨿ B).Modules`.  Project-local leaf of the Stub-2 chain. -/
725	private theorem isIso_coprodDecompMap (M : (A ⨿ B).Modules) :
726	    IsIso (coprodDecompMap M) := by
727	  rw [Scheme.Modules.Hom.isIso_iff_isIso_app]
728	  intro U
729	  set ι₀ : A ⟶ A ⨿ B := coprod.inl with hι₀
730	  set ι₁ : B ⟶ A ⨿ B := coprod.inr with hι₁
731	  set W₀ : (A ⨿ B).Opens := ι₀ ''ᵁ ι₀ ⁻¹ᵁ U with hW₀
732	  set W₁ : (A ⨿ B).Opens := ι₁ ''ᵁ ι₁ ⁻¹ᵁ U with hW₁
733	  have h₂ : W₀ ⊓ W₁ = ⊥ := by
734	    rw [hW₀, hW₁]
735	    simp_rw [Scheme.Hom.image_preimage_eq_opensRange_inf]
736	    rw [← inf_inf_distrib_right, (isCompl_opensRange_inl_inr A B).inf_eq_bot, bot_inf_eq]
737	  have h₁ : W₀ ⊔ W₁ = U := by
738	    rw [hW₀, hW₁]
739	    simp_rw [Scheme.Hom.image_preimage_eq_opensRange_inf]
740	    rw [← inf_sup_right, (isCompl_opensRange_inl_inr A B).sup_eq_top, top_inf_eq]
741	  -- the two restriction-unit legs at `U`, identified as presheaf restriction maps
742	  set u₀ := (Scheme.Modules.restrictAdjunction ι₀).unit.app M with hu₀
743	  set u₁ := (Scheme.Modules.restrictAdjunction ι₁).unit.app M with hu₁
744	  have hleg₀ : u₀.app U = M.presheaf.map (homOfLE (ι₀.image_preimage_le U)).op := by
745	    rw [hu₀, Scheme.Modules.restrictAdjunction_unit_app_app]
746	  have hleg₁ : u₁.app U = M.presheaf.map (homOfLE (ι₁.image_preimage_le U)).op := by
747	    rw [hu₁, Scheme.Modules.restrictAdjunction_unit_app_app]
748	  -- the disjoint-union limit on the underlying abelian sheaf of `M`, transported to `Γ(M, U)`
749	  have LimAb : IsLimit (BinaryFan.mk (u₀.app U) (u₁.app U)) := by
750	    have L := TopCat.Sheaf.isProductOfDisjoint
751	      (⟨M.presheaf, M.isSheaf⟩ : TopCat.Sheaf Ab _) W₀ W₁ h₂
752	    refine L.ofIsoLimit (Cone.ext (M.presheaf.mapIso (eqToIso (congrArg Opposite.op h₁))) ?_)
753	    rintro ⟨(_ | _)⟩
754	    · show M.presheaf.map (homOfLE le_sup_left).op
755	        = M.presheaf.map (eqToHom (congrArg Opposite.op h₁)) ≫ u₀.app U
756	      rw [hleg₀, show (homOfLE (le_sup_left : W₀ ≤ W₀ ⊔ W₁)).op
757	            = eqToHom (congrArg Opposite.op h₁) ≫ (homOfLE (ι₀.image_preimage_le U)).op
758	          from Subsingleton.elim _ _, M.presheaf.map_comp]
759	      rfl
760	    · show M.presheaf.map (homOfLE le_sup_right).op
761	        = M.presheaf.map (eqToHom (congrArg Opposite.op h₁)) ≫ u₁.app U
762	      rw [hleg₁, show (homOfLE (le_sup_right : W₁ ≤ W₀ ⊔ W₁)).op
763	            = eqToHom (congrArg Opposite.op h₁) ≫ (homOfLE (ι₁.image_preimage_le U)).op
764	          from Subsingleton.elim _ _, M.presheaf.map_comp]
765	      rfl
766	  -- reflect the limit up to `ModuleCat` through the evaluation functor
767	  set G := SheafOfModules.evaluation (R := (A ⨿ B).ringCatSheaf) (Opposite.op U) with hGdef
768	  have LimMod : IsLimit (BinaryFan.mk (G.map u₀) (G.map u₁)) := by
769	    refine isLimitOfReflectsOfMapIsLimit (forget₂ (ModuleCat _) AddCommGrpCat)
770	      (G.map u₀) (G.map u₁) ?_
771	    exact LimAb
772	  -- conclude isomorphy of the comparison map at `U`
773	  haveI key : IsIso (G.map (coprodDecompMap M)) :=
774	    isIso_map_prodLift_of_isLimit G LimMod
775	  have happ : (coprodDecompMap M).app U
776	      = (forget₂ (ModuleCat _) AddCommGrpCat).map (G.map (coprodDecompMap M)) := rfl
777	  rw [happ]
778	  exact Functor.map_isIso _ _

781	
782	The binary disjoint-cover decomposition `isIso_coprodDecompMap` is upgraded to a statement about
783	the push–pull object `pushPullObj F (Over.mk q)` (`q = coprod.desc pA pB`).  The single substantive
784	node is the per-leg coherence `pushPull_binary_leg_coherence` (★): the contravariant push–pull map of

790	/-- The canonical leg iso identifying `q_*(c_*(M.restrict c))` (a factor of `q_*(P ⨯ Q)`, where
791	`M = q^* F` and `c` is a coproduct inclusion `coprod.inl`/`coprod.inr`) with the push–pull object
792	`pushPullObj F (Over.mk pC)` along the over-triangle `wC : c ≫ q = pC`.  Project-local component of
793	the L2 `q_*`-coherence chain. -/
794	private noncomputable def pushPullCoprodLegIso {C : Scheme.{u}} (q : (A ⨿ B) ⟶ X)
795	    (c : C ⟶ A ⨿ B) [IsOpenImmersion c] (pC : C ⟶ X) (wC : c ≫ q = pC) (F : X.Modules) :
796	    (pushforward q).obj ((pushforward c).obj
797	        (((Scheme.Modules.pullback q).obj F).restrict c)) ≅
798	      pushPullObj F (Over.mk pC) :=
799	  (pushforward q).mapIso ((pushforward c).mapIso
800	    ((Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctorIsoPullback c).app ((Scheme.Modules.pullback q).obj F) ≪≫
801	      (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp c q).app F ≪≫
802	      (Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr wC).app F)) ≪≫
803	  eqToIso (congrArg (fun p => (pushforward p).obj ((Scheme.Modules.pullback pC).obj F)) wC)

806	-- concrete pushforward/pullback objects, whose `whnf` exceeds the default heartbeat budget.
807	set_option maxHeartbeats 800000 in
808	/-- (★) Per-leg coherence: the push–pull map of the over-inclusion `Over.homMk c : Over.mk pC ⟶
809	Over.mk q` is, through the canonical leg iso, the pushforward of the disjoint-cover restriction unit
810	`(restrictAdjunction c).unit`.  This is the bridge that converts the canonical comparison map
811	`prod.lift (pushPullMap F …)` into the manifestly-iso `coprodDecompMap` chain.  Project-local. -/
812	private lemma pushPull_binary_leg_coherence {C : Scheme.{u}} (q : (A ⨿ B) ⟶ X)
813	    (c : C ⟶ A ⨿ B) [IsOpenImmersion c] (pC : C ⟶ X) (wC : c ≫ q = pC) (F : X.Modules) :
814	    pushPullMap F (Over.homMk c wC : Over.mk pC ⟶ Over.mk q) =
815	      (pushforward q).map
816	          ((Scheme.Modules.restrictAdjunction c).unit.app ((Scheme.Modules.pullback q).obj F)) ≫
817	        (pushPullCoprodLegIso q c pC wC F).hom := by
818	  have hraw : pushPullMap F (Over.homMk c wC : Over.mk pC ⟶ Over.mk q)
819	      = rawPushPullMap c q pC wC F := rfl
820	  rw [hraw, rawPushPullMap_self_gen]
821	  have hLAU : (Scheme.Modules.restrictAdjunction c).unit.app ((Scheme.Modules.pullback q).obj F) ≫
822	        (pushforward c).map
823	          ((Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctorIsoPullback c).hom.app
824	            ((Scheme.Modules.pullback q).obj F)) =
825	      (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction c).unit.app
826	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback q).obj F) :=
827	    Adjunction.unit_leftAdjointUniq_hom_app _ _ _
828	  subst wC
829	  simp only [pushPullCoprodLegIso, Iso.trans_hom, Functor.mapIso_hom, eqToIso.hom,
830	    Iso.app_hom, Category.comp_id,
831	    Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr, eqToIso_refl, Iso.refl_hom, NatTrans.id_app]
832	  rw [← hLAU]
833	  simp only [Functor.map_comp, Category.assoc]; rfl

839	(the mandatory framing the downstream section-identification needs); it is shown to be an
840	isomorphism by matching it leg-by-leg, via the per-leg coherence
841	`pushPull_binary_leg_coherence` (★), against the manifestly-invertible reference chain through the
842	binary disjoint-union decomposition `coprodDecompMap`.  Project-local L2 assembly
843	(blueprint `lem:pushPull_binary_coprod_prod`). -/

845	    pushPullObj F (Over.mk (Limits.coprod.desc Y₀.hom Y₁.hom)) ≅
846	      pushPullObj F Y₀ ⨯ pushPullObj F Y₁ := by
847	  set q : Y₀.left ⨿ Y₁.left ⟶ X := Limits.coprod.desc Y₀.hom Y₁.hom with hq
848	  set M := (Scheme.Modules.pullback q).obj F with hM
849	  have wInl : (Limits.coprod.inl : Y₀.left ⟶ _) ≫ q = Y₀.hom := Limits.coprod.inl_desc _ _
850	  have wInr : (Limits.coprod.inr : Y₁.left ⟶ _) ≫ q = Y₁.hom := Limits.coprod.inr_desc _ _
851	  set overInl : Y₀ ⟶ Over.mk q := Over.homMk Limits.coprod.inl wInl with hoverInl
852	  set overInr : Y₁ ⟶ Over.mk q := Over.homMk Limits.coprod.inr wInr with hoverInr
853	  haveI : IsIso (coprodDecompMap M) := isIso_coprodDecompMap M
854	  -- The per-leg identifications.  Their codomains are pinned to `pushPullObj F Y₀`/`Y₁`
855	  -- (defeq to the `pushPullObj F (Over.mk Y₀.hom)` produced by `pushPullCoprodLegIso`); the

857	  -- the trailing `prod.fst` on `pushPullObj F Y₀ ⨯ pushPullObj F Y₁`.

859	      ≅ pushPullObj F Y₀ :=
860	    pushPullCoprodLegIso q Limits.coprod.inl Y₀.hom wInl F with hidiso0
861	  set idiso₁ : (pushforward q).obj ((pushforward Limits.coprod.inr).obj (M.restrict Limits.coprod.inr))
862	      ≅ pushPullObj F Y₁ :=
863	    pushPullCoprodLegIso q Limits.coprod.inr Y₁.hom wInr F with hidiso1
864	  have hcoh0 : pushPullMap F overInl
865	      = (pushforward q).map ((Scheme.Modules.restrictAdjunction Limits.coprod.inl).unit.app M)
866	          ≫ idiso₀.hom := by
867	    rw [hidiso0]; exact pushPull_binary_leg_coherence q Limits.coprod.inl Y₀.hom wInl F
868	  have hcoh1 : pushPullMap F overInr
869	      = (pushforward q).map ((Scheme.Modules.restrictAdjunction Limits.coprod.inr).unit.app M)
870	          ≫ idiso₁.hom := by
871	    rw [hidiso1]; exact pushPull_binary_leg_coherence q Limits.coprod.inr Y₁.hom wInr F
872	  set chainIso : (pushforward q).obj M ≅ pushPullObj F Y₀ ⨯ pushPullObj F Y₁ :=
873	    (pushforward q).mapIso (asIso (coprodDecompMap M)) ≪≫
874	      Limits.PreservesLimitPair.iso (pushforward q) _ _ ≪≫
875	      Limits.prod.mapIso idiso₀ idiso₁ with hchain
876	  -- Match the canonical comparison against the reference chain entirely through `prod.lift`
877	  -- identities (`prod.lift_map`, `prod.comp_lift`, and `prodComparison = prod.lift (q_* fst)

881	    rw [hcoh0, hcoh1, hchain, Iso.trans_hom, Iso.trans_hom, Functor.mapIso_hom, asIso_hom,

883	    show Limits.prod.lift _ _ =
884	      (pushforward q).map (coprodDecompMap M) ≫
885	        Limits.prod.lift ((pushforward q).map Limits.prod.fst) ((pushforward q).map Limits.prod.snd)

889	    rfl
890	  haveI : IsIso (Limits.prod.lift (pushPullMap F overInl) (pushPullMap F overInr)) := by

914	/-- Push–pull objects transport along a slice isomorphism `e : Y ≅ Y'` in `Over X`.  The
915	push–pull object is a contravariant functor of its slice argument, so the forward map is
916	`pushPullMap F e.inv` and the backward map `pushPullMap F e.hom`.  Project-local
917	(blueprint `lem:pushPullObjCongr`). -/
918	noncomputable def pushPullObjCongr (F : X.Modules) {Y Y' : Over X} (e : Y ≅ Y') :
919	    pushPullObj F Y ≅ pushPullObj F Y' where
920	  hom := pushPullMap F e.inv
921	  inv := pushPullMap F e.hom
922	  hom_inv_id := by rw [← pushPullMap_comp, e.hom_inv_id, pushPullMap_id]

929	(blueprint `lem:over_sigmaOptionIso`). -/
930	noncomputable def overSigmaOptionIso {α : Type*} (legs : Option α → Over X)
931	    [HasCoproduct (fun o => (legs o).left)]
932	    [HasCoproduct (fun a : α => (legs (some a)).left)]
933	    [HasBinaryCoproduct (legs none).left (∐ fun a : α => (legs (some a)).left)] :
934	    Over.mk (Limits.Sigma.desc (fun o => (legs o).hom)) ≅
935	      Over.mk (Limits.coprod.desc (legs none).hom
936	        (Limits.Sigma.desc (fun a : α => (legs (some a)).hom))) :=
937	  Over.isoMk (sigmaOptionIso (fun o => (legs o).left)) (by
938	    refine Limits.Sigma.hom_ext _ _ (fun o => ?_)
939	    rcases o with _ | a
940	    · simp only [sigmaOptionIso, Over.mk_left, Over.mk_hom,

949	    [HasBinaryProduct (W none) (∏ᶜ fun a : α => W (some a))] :
950	    (∏ᶜ W) ≅ W none ⨯ (∏ᶜ fun a : α => W (some a)) where
951	  hom := Limits.prod.lift (Limits.Pi.π W none)
952	    (Limits.Pi.lift (fun a => Limits.Pi.π W (some a)))
953	  inv := Limits.Pi.lift (fun o => Option.rec Limits.prod.fst
954	    (fun a => Limits.prod.snd ≫ Limits.Pi.π (fun a : α => W (some a)) a) o)

956	    apply Limits.Pi.hom_ext
957	    rintro (_ | a)
958	    · simp only [Category.assoc, Limits.Pi.lift_π, Limits.prod.lift_fst, Category.id_comp]
959	    · rw [Category.id_comp, Category.assoc, Limits.Pi.lift_π, ← Category.assoc,
960	        Limits.prod.lift_snd, Limits.Pi.lift_π]
961	  inv_hom_id := by
962	    apply Limits.prod.hom_ext
963	    · simp only [Category.assoc, Limits.prod.lift_fst, Limits.Pi.lift_π, Category.id_comp]
964	    · apply Limits.Pi.hom_ext
965	      intro a
966	      simp only [Category.assoc, Limits.prod.lift_snd, Limits.Pi.lift_π, Category.id_comp]

994	  ext U x

996	    have h : Subsingleton ↑Γ(M, U) := Module.subsingleton ↑Γ(Z, U) _

1000	/-- Empty base case of the finite induction: for the empty index the comparison map
1001	`coprodToProdMap` is an isomorphism (both source and target are terminal — the push–pull
1002	of the initial scheme and the empty product).  Project-local
1003	(blueprint `lem:pushPull_coprod_prod_empty`). -/
1004	private theorem pushPull_coprod_prod_empty (F : X.Modules) (legs : PEmpty.{u + 1} → Over X) :
1005	    IsIso (coprodToProdMap F legs) := by
1006	  -- Target: the empty product is terminal.
1007	  have hY : Limits.IsTerminal (∏ᶜ fun i : PEmpty.{u + 1} => pushPullObj F (legs i)) :=
1008	    Limits.IsTerminal.ofUniqueHom (fun _ => Limits.Pi.lift (fun i => i.elim))
1009	      (fun _ m => Limits.Pi.hom_ext _ _ (fun i => i.elim))
1010	  -- Source: push–pull of the initial scheme.  `pushforward q` is a right adjoint, so it
1011	  -- preserves the terminal object; and `(pullback q).obj F` is terminal over the initial
1012	  -- scheme (its structure sheaf has zero sections, so every module sheaf is terminal).
1013	  have hX : Limits.IsTerminal (pushPullObj F
1014	      (Over.mk (Limits.Sigma.desc (fun i : PEmpty.{u + 1} => (legs i).hom)))) := by
1015	    -- `pushPullObj F Y = (pushforward Y.hom).obj ((pullback Y.hom).obj F)`; pushforward preserves
1016	    -- zero objects (it is additive), and the pulled-back module lives over the initial scheme
1017	    -- `∐ PEmpty`, whose only sheaf of modules is zero.
1018	    refine (CategoryTheory.Functor.map_isZero (Scheme.Modules.pushforward _) ?_).isTerminal
